title: DSPy Monty Interpreter Tour
description: Explore the architecture of a secure, microsecond-startup DSPy CodeInterpreter backed by a Rust-based Python VM

steps.title: Project Overview
steps.description: Welcome to dspy-monty-interpreter! This package provides a DSPy CodeInterpreter implementation backed by Monty, a secure Python interpreter written in Rust. It delivers microsecond startup times, sandboxed execution (no filesystem/network access), and state persistence across calls — all without subprocess or WASM overhead.

interactiveActions.description: Look around the project — it's intentionally compact with a clean separation of source, tests, and benchmarks

steps.title: Core Implementation
steps.description: The entire interpreter lives in a single module. interpreter.py contains the MontyInterpreter class (325 lines) which implements DSPy's CodeInterpreter protocol. The __init__.py re-exports MontyInterpreter as the public API. The key design: code is accumulated across execute calls, replayed with cached tool results, and only new output is returned.

interactiveActions.description: Click interpreter.py to see the MontyInterpreter class — the execute() method is the heart of the system

steps.title: State Accumulation & Tool Dispatch
steps.description: MontyInterpreter's defining pattern is state accumulation with replay. Each successful execute call appends code to _code_history. On the next call, all prior code is replayed with a boundary marker separating old from new. Tool calls (registered via the tools dict) pause the Rust VM, invoke host-side Python functions, and cache results so replays never re-trigger side effects. The SUBMIT built-in returns FinalOutput to signal completion to the RLM.

steps.title: Test Suite
steps.description: The test suite provides comprehensive coverage of every architectural invariant: protocol conformance, state persistence, SUBMIT handling, tool dispatch and caching, print suppression during replay, error recovery, and RLM compatibility.

interactiveActions.description: Click test_interpreter.py to see how the replay, caching, and tool dispatch behaviors are validated

steps.title: Performance Benchmarks
steps.description: bench.py compares MontyInterpreter against DSPy's built-in PythonInterpreter across arithmetic, string ops, recursion, sorting, state accumulation, tool calls, and SUBMIT workflows. It measures startup time, per-task execution, and shutdown, supporting multiple runs with median aggregation and JSON output for automation. This is where the microsecond startup advantage is quantified.

interactiveActions.description: Click bench.py to see the performance comparison methodology

steps.title: Configuration & Packaging
steps.description: pyproject.toml defines the package metadata, dependencies, and build system (hatchling). The .github/monty-version.txt tracks the known compatible Monty VM version. Built distributions (wheel and tarball) live in dist/. The package supports Python 3.10–3.13 and is published under the MIT license.

interactiveActions.description: Click pyproject.toml to see dependencies and package configuration
